headline: Syariah-civil jurisdiction clash affects 4,200 families
context: Unilateral child conversion disputes and custody battles between syariah and civil courts affected over 4,200 families as of 2025.

cards.text: 4,200 Malaysian families caught between syariah and civil court jurisdictions
cards.sub: Conflicting rulings leave children and spouses without clear legal resolution

cards.h: Federal Constitution Article 121(1A) bars civil courts from syariah matters
cards.s: Indira Gandhi 2018 ruling limited unilateral conversion but gaps remain
cards.d: Federal Constitution Article 121(1A) bars civil courts from syariah matters Indira Gandhi 2018 ruling limited unilateral conversion but gaps remain

cards.h: Both Article 11 and Article 12(4) of Constitution protect religious freedom
cards.s: Children's right to religion intersects with parental authority in complex ways
cards.d: Both Article 11 and Article 12(4) of Constitution protect religious freedom Children's right to religion intersects with parental authority in complex ways

cards.h: M. Indira Gandhi waited 11 years before Federal Court resolved her case
cards.s: Prolonged litigation devastates families emotionally and financially
cards.d: M. Indira Gandhi waited 11 years before Federal Court resolved her case Prolonged litigation devastates families emotionally and financially

cards.text: Dual jurisdiction without coordination means families fall between two systems
Like two hospitals with different treatment protocols for the same patient. The patient suffers while the doctors argue jurisdiction.
cards.sub: Families caught between syariah and civil courts face contradictory rulings with no mechanism for resolution

cards.text: Law Reform Committee proposed mediation framework for dual-court disputes in 2023
